title: Pre-warm cancellation: clean shutdown via threading.Event
description: Ensure the pre-warm pipeline (TASK-027) can be interrupted cleanly on uvicorn shutdown: no partial rows committed, no dangling threads, no exceptions logged.
acceptanceCriteria: On uvicorn SIGTERM/SIGINT: app.state.warmup_cancel_event is set; the daemon thread exits within 5 seconds.
Partial cache rows already written are valid (per-row commits are atomic).
tests/test_warmup.py covers: start warm-up, set cancel event mid-cache-step, assert thread join within 1s.
If pyrekordbox DB calls in flight at cancellation, they finish gracefully (no force-kill).

steps.details: serve/deps.py:lifespan after 'yield', set app.state.warmup_cancel_event.set(); call shutdown_pool() (from TASK-001); join the warm-up thread with timeout=5.

Per TASK-018, warm_up already checks cancel_event between iterations. Verify at the writer-thread boundary that the cancel check happens after each batch commit, not mid-batch (so partial commits don't lose data).

tests/test_warmup.py: spawn warm-up against a 100-track mock; set cancel after 20 processed; assert thread.join(timeout=1) returns; assert CacheStore has rows for the 20 processed and 0 for the rest. No exceptions raised.

technicalNotes: Daemon threads die with the process anyway, but graceful shutdown matters for: (a) signal-handler cleanliness in tests, (b) leaving CacheStore in a consistent state (committed transactions only).
If the 5s join timeout expires, log a warning (don't raise) - the process is exiting anyway.
